scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: early tolerance and metabolic response review

legacy_compatibility.notes: Legacy simplified fields can be generated downstream if needed.

dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: Semaglutide is intended for continuous use, not cycling. Discontinuation leads to weight regain (~6.9% over 1 year per STEP 4). Maintenance dose should be sustained indefinitely if tolerated.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: 2.4 mg subcutaneous once weekly (target dose). Compounded versions may use equivalent weekly dose via subcutaneous or oral routes.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: Administer once weekly on same day each week. May be taken at any time of day. Morning administration preferred for consistency. No food requirement.
dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Start at 0.25 mg/week subcutaneous for weeks 1-4. Increase to 0.5 mg/week weeks 5-8; 1.0 mg/week weeks 9-12; 1.7 mg/week weeks 13-16; 2.4 mg/week from week 17. Slow titration minimizes GI adverse events.

adverse_events_common: Nausea: 44% (most common; transient weeks 1-8, resolves with titration)
Vomiting: 24% - dose-dependent, mitigated by slow titration
Injection site reactions: 5-10% - mild, rotate sites
adverse_events_serious: Acute pancreatitis: rare (<0.5%); stop if severe abdominal pain occurs
Gallbladder disease (cholelithiasis): ~2.6x increased risk vs placebo (STEP 1)
Thyroid C-cell hyperplasia: rodent signal; contraindicated with MTC or MEN2 history
Resting heart rate increase: ~3 bpm average; monitor in cardiac patients
drug_interactions: Delays gastric emptying - alters absorption of oral narrow therapeutic index drugs
Insulin or sulfonylureas: increased hypoglycemia risk - dose reduction may be needed
Warfarin: monitor INR - absorption timing changes possible
Oral contraceptives: take 1 hour before or 4 hours after semaglutide
monitoring_required: Body weight and BMI at baseline, week 4, 8, 12, 16
Fasting glucose and HbA1c at baseline and week 12
Lipid panel (LDL, HDL, TG) at baseline and week 16
Serum amylase/lipase if abdominal pain occurs
TSH at baseline if clinically indicated
Blood pressure and resting heart rate at each visit

metadata.longDescription: This 12-week protocol integrates tirzepatide (a glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide [GIP] and glucagon-like peptide-1 [GLP-1] receptor dual agonist) with MOTS-c (a mitochondrial open reading frame of the 12S rRNA-c peptide). Tirzepatide achieves dose-dependent reductions in HbA1c and body weight via hypothalamic satiety signaling and enhanced insulin secretion (Frias et al., NEJM 2021; Jastreboff et al., NEJM 2022). MOTS-c, an endogenous mitochondria-derived peptide, activates AMPK and the folate-methionine cycle to improve exercise capacity and metabolic flexibility (Lee et al., Cell Metab 2015). The protocol progresses through escalation and maintenance sub-phases to minimise GI adverse events while maximising adipose reduction.

references.citation: Frias JP et al. Tirzepatide versus Semaglutide. NEJM 2021;385:503-515.
Jastreboff AM et al. Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity. NEJM 2022;387:205-216.
Lee C et al. The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis. Cell Metab 2015;21:443-454.

metadata.scientificName: Tirzepatide + MOTS-c GLP-1/GIP Dual-Agonist & Mitokine Protocol
metadata.description: 12-week structured metabolic protocol combining tirzepatide GLP-1/GIP dual agonism with MOTS-c mitochondrial signaling for progressive fat loss and insulin sensitivity.
metadata.clinical_summary: Structured metabolic weight-management protocol centered on tirzepatide-driven GLP-1/GIP appetite and insulin signaling, supported by mitochondrial and lipolytic adjuncts when clinically appropriate. The protocol is designed to improve satiety, insulin sensitivity, body composition, and metabolic flexibility through phased initiation, escalation, and maintenance.
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Strong clinical evidence exists for tirzepatide in obesity and metabolic disease. Adjunct peptide components have lower, mostly translational evidence; therefore the combined protocol is graded A- rather than A.

wm_001.overview_summary: Three-phase structured metabolic weight reduction protocol using GLP-based support and mitochondrial optimization.
wm_001.protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Reduced appetite
Progressive fat loss
Improved metabolic efficiency

quantitative_ranges.week_4: Early appetite reduction and 1-3% body-weight reduction depending on adherence and baseline BMI.
quantitative_ranges.week_8: Typical 3-6% body-weight reduction and measurable waist circumference improvement.
quantitative_ranges.week_12: Expected 5-10% body-weight reduction in responders; larger reductions may require longer titration windows.

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

contraindications: Personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (GLP-1 class)

side_effects.management: Rotate injection sites; use 29–31G insulin needles; apply cold compress.
Administer with food; reduce dose temporarily if persistent.
Adjust injection timing; evaluate sleep hygiene.
Hydration; paracetamol as needed; review dosing.

escalation_criteria: Allergic reaction (urticaria, angioedema, anaphylaxis) — STOP immediately, administer epinephrine if severe
Persistent injection site infection (redness, warmth, pus) — STOP, antibiotics, dermatology review
Significant mood deterioration or psychotic symptoms — STOP, psychiatric referral
Unexplained abnormal labs > 2× upper limit of normal — STOP, specialist review
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 16-week active protocol (divided as 8-week primary + 8-week consolidation). 4-6 week washout before repeat cycle. AOD-9604 receptor sensitivity maintained with cycling; continuous use beyond 20 weeks not recommended.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: AOD-9604: 300 mcg/day SC (pre-breakfast, fasted). 5-Amino-1MQ (if included): 50-100 mg orally 2x/day. Fragment 176-191 (if used): 500 mcg/day SC on training days.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: AOD-9604: administer 30 minutes before breakfast in fasted state for maximal lipolytic effect via GHSR-independent beta-3 adrenergic activation. Avoid carbohydrate intake within 30 minutes post-injection to preserve insulin-independent fat mobilization.
dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Weeks 1-2: AOD-9604 at 150 mcg/day SC to assess tolerability. Weeks 3-8: increase to 300 mcg/day. Weeks 9-16: maintain 300 mcg/day with caloric deficit protocol. 5-Amino-1MQ can be added from week 3 at 50 mg 2x/day to boost NNMT inhibition and adipocyte metabolic rate.

qualitative: Progressive reduction in visceral and subcutaneous adipose tissue
Improved lipid panel: reduction in LDL and triglycerides
Reduced appetite and improved satiety signaling
Preservation of lean mass during caloric deficit
Improved fasting glucose and insulin sensitivity

quantitative_ranges.week_16: Total body fat reduction of 8-14% with caloric deficit; visceral fat area reduction 15-25% (Heffernan 2001)
quantitative_ranges.week_4: Weight loss of 1.5-3 kg vs baseline; waist circumference reduction of 2-4 cm; appetite VAS improvement 20-35%
quantitative_ranges.week_8: Total body fat reduction 4-6%; LDL reduction 8-12% from baseline; fasting insulin decrease 15-20%

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 65-75% for fat loss greater than 5% body fat; ~60% for lipid normalization

adverse_events_common: Mild injection site reactions with AOD-9604 SC (10-15%)
Transient nausea in first 1-2 weeks at higher doses (5-10%)
Mild hypoglycemia risk if combined with insulin sensitizers - monitor glucose
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events in AOD-9604 Phase II/III trials (Heffernan 2001, Baker 2009)
5-Amino-1MQ: insufficient long-term human safety data - use with informed consent
drug_interactions: AOD-9604 does not stimulate GH axis - safe to co-administer with GH-releasing peptides
Avoid high carbohydrate meals within 30 min of AOD-9604 injection (insulin antagonizes lipolytic effect)
5-Amino-1MQ may interact with methylation-pathway medications - caution with folate antagonists
monitoring_required: Body composition (DXA or BIA) at baseline and week 8/16
Fasting glucose, HbA1c, fasting insulin at weeks 0 and 8
Lipid panel at weeks 0 and 12
Weight and waist circumference monthly

metadata.longDescription: This protocol pairs semaglutide (a GLP-1 receptor agonist approved for chronic weight management) with cagrilintide (a long-acting amylin analogue). Semaglutide reduces energy intake via hypothalamic GLP-1R activation and slows gastric emptying (Wilding et al., NEJM 2021). Cagrilintide targets amylin receptors in the hypothalamus and area postrema, providing complementary satiety signalling beyond GLP-1 pathways (Enebo et al., Lancet 2021). The REDEFINE-1 trial of the fixed-dose combination (CagriSema) demonstrated superior weight reduction vs. either monotherapy (Knop et al., Lancet 2023). AOD-9604 is added in the escalation phase to enhance lipolysis via truncated GH fragment activity without promoting insulin resistance.

references.citation: Wilding JPH et al. Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity. NEJM 2021;384:989-1002.
Enebo LB et al. Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of cagrilintide. Lancet 2021;397:2351-2363.
Knop FK et al. Semaglutide and Cagrilintide (CagriSema). Lancet 2023;402:2395-2407.

metadata.scientificName: Semaglutide + Cagrilintide GLP-1 & Long-Acting Amylin Dual-Hormone Protocol
metadata.description: 12-week dual-hormone weight-loss protocol pairing semaglutide GLP-1 agonism with cagrilintide amylin receptor activation for sustained appetite suppression and adiposity reduction.

data.protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.
data.overview_summary: Three-phase weight management protocol combining semaglutide-based escalation with cagrilintide support to intensify appetite and weight-control signaling.

metadata.clinical_summary: Dual-hormone weight-management protocol combining GLP-1 receptor agonism with long-acting amylin signaling to improve satiety, reduce energy intake, and support progressive adiposity reduction. AOD-9604 is included as a secondary lipolytic support layer during escalation.
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Semaglutide has strong clinical evidence for obesity. Cagrilintide and CagriSema data are clinically promising but still evolving; AOD-9604 has weaker adjunctive evidence.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Assess early tolerance, adherence, and initial metabolic response.
Assess metabolic direction and adjust tempo if needed.
Assess outcome, safety, and continuation or washout decision.

qualitative: Improved appetite control through complementary GLP-1 and amylin pathways
Progressive reduction in total and visceral adiposity
Improved glycemic and lipid markers in metabolic responders
Better satiety durability than GLP-1 monotherapy in selected patients
Support for body-composition improvement when paired with structured lifestyle guidance

quantitative_ranges.week_4: Early appetite suppression and tolerability signal; 1-3% body-weight reduction possible.
quantitative_ranges.week_8: 3-6% body-weight reduction in responders with improved satiety metrics.
quantitative_ranges.week_12: 5-10% body-weight reduction possible; longer programs may produce greater reductions.

dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 16-week body recomposition cycle: 8 weeks anabolic emphasis (Ipamorelin + CJC-1295), then 8 weeks lipolytic emphasis (AOD-9604 + MK-677 taper). 6-week washout required. Maximum 2 cycles per year.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: Ipamorelin: 200-300 mcg SC 3x/day (pre-meal + pre-sleep). CJC-1295 DAC: 2 mg SC 1x/week. AOD-9604: 250-300 mcg/day SC fasted (added at week 8 in lipolytic phase).
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: Ipamorelin: pre-meal injections leverage GH pulse amplification during insulin-independent windows. Pre-sleep dose maximizes nocturnal GH surge for anabolic recovery. CJC-1295 DAC weekly injection on rest days to avoid blunting acute GH peaks.
dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Weeks 1-2: Ipamorelin 100 mcg SC 3x/day. Weeks 3-8: increase to 200-300 mcg 3x/day. CJC-1295 DAC 1 mg/week from week 2, increase to 2 mg/week by week 4. Add AOD-9604 300 mcg/day from week 9 for lipolytic phase intensification.

adverse_events_common: Transient water retention in first 2-4 weeks (15-20%)
Transient headache or flushing post-Ipamorelin injection (5-10%)
Mild cortisol blunting effect - monitor energy and stress resilience
adverse_events_serious: Elevated IGF-1 beyond 350 ng/mL - dose reduction required; monitor for acromegalic features
Insulin resistance worsening possible with prolonged high-dose GH stimulation - monitor fasting glucose
drug_interactions: Avoid concurrent GH replacement therapy - additive effect may cause IGF-1 supraphysiological elevation
Corticosteroids blunt GH secretagogue response - avoid concurrent use
AOD-9604 is GH-axis-independent and safe to stack with Ipamorelin/CJC-1295
monitoring_required: IGF-1 serum at baseline, week 8, and week 16
Fasting glucose and HbA1c at weeks 0, 8, 16
DXA or BIA body composition at baseline and week 8/16
Blood pressure and resting heart rate monthly
Thyroid function (TSH) at baseline and end of cycle

metadata.longDescription: This advanced protocol bridges metabolic weight management with longevity biology by combining GLP-1 receptor agonism (appetite and insulin regulation) with mitochondrial peptides (MOTS-c) that activate AMPK, improve insulin sensitivity, and modulate the folate cycle. The compound selection targets the intersection of metabolic syndrome and accelerated cellular aging: excess adiposity promotes senescence, systemic inflammation, and mitochondrial dysfunction. By addressing these pathways concurrently, the protocol aims for body-composition remodeling alongside hallmarks-of-aging improvements (López-Otín et al., Cell 2023). Appropriate for patients with metabolic syndrome who have failed monotherapy approaches.

references.citation: López-Otín C et al. Hallmarks of aging: An expanding universe. Cell 2023;186:243-278.
Lee C et al. The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis. Cell Metab 2015;21:443-454.
Jastreboff AM et al. Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity. NEJM 2022;387:205-216.

metadata.description: Advanced multi-compound weight and longevity protocol combining GLP-1-class agonism with mitochondrial peptides for body-composition remodeling and cellular rejuvenation.

data.protocol_source_reference: Advanced Metabolic & Longevity Protocol — 16 Week Clinical Program
data.overview_summary: Intensive 16-week metabolic optimization and fat loss program focusing on visceral fat reduction and mitochondrial priming.

metadata.clinical_summary: High-complexity metabolic and longevity-oriented body-composition protocol combining triple-agonist weight management with mitochondrial, GH-axis, NAD, and tissue-support peptides. The protocol targets visceral adiposity, insulin sensitivity, mitochondrial resilience, and recovery capacity through priming, active fat-loss, and stabilization phases.
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Retatrutide and mitochondrial peptide components have promising clinical or translational evidence, but the complete multi-compound stack is not validated as a single standardized regimen.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Assess early tolerance, adherence, and initial metabolic response.
Assess metabolic direction and adjust tempo if needed.
Assess outcome, safety, and continuation or washout decision.

qualitative: Significant visceral fat reduction in responders
Improved insulin sensitivity and metabolic flexibility
Enhanced mitochondrial function and fatigue resilience
Improved body-composition remodeling
Better recovery capacity during active fat-loss phases

quantitative_ranges.week_4: Early tolerance and appetite/metabolic signal; 1-3% body-weight reduction possible.
quantitative_ranges.week_8: Meaningful adiposity and body-composition shift; monitor IGF-1, glucose, and tolerance.
quantitative_ranges.week_16: Potential 8-15% body-weight reduction in responders with improved waist circumference and metabolic markers.

dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week ongoing maintenance protocol with quarterly reassessment. No mandatory washout required given maintenance-dose compound selection. Discontinue if weight regain exceeds 5% body mass despite adherence.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: AOD-9604: 150 mcg/day SC 5 days/week (not daily, to prevent receptor desensitization). GLP-1 analog (low-dose maintenance): 0.5 mg weekly SC (Semaglutide) or equivalent. Optional: BPC-157 300 mcg 3x/week for gut integrity and metabolic recovery support.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: AOD-9604: morning fasted injection 5 days/week. Semaglutide maintenance dose: fixed day weekly injection, evening preferred to reduce nausea impact during waking hours. BPC-157 (if included): separate injection, morning or post-meal.
dosing_enrichment.titration_note: This protocol assumes prior completion of an active weight reduction phase (wm_001 or wm_002). Begin at 50% of prior active doses. Week 1-4: AOD-9604 150 mcg/day + Semaglutide 0.5 mg/week. Week 4+: maintain if weight stable. Increase AOD-9604 to 250 mcg/day if weight creep is detected.

adverse_events_common: Mild GI symptoms persisting from active GLP-1 phase (5-10% at maintenance dose)
Injection site fatigue with AOD-9604 long-term use - rotate sites diligently
Reduced appetite may require intentional protein intake to maintain lean mass
adverse_events_serious: GLP-1 continuation carries pancreatitis risk (0.1-0.3%) - monitor amylase/lipase if abdominal pain occurs
Thyroid C-cell tumor signal in rodent studies with GLP-1 agonists - contraindicated in MEN2 or medullary thyroid carcinoma history
drug_interactions: GLP-1 maintenance slows gastric emptying - monitor oral medication absorption timing
AOD-9604 maintenance dose is GH-axis-neutral - no interaction with concurrent peptide protocols
BPC-157 adjunct: no known drug interactions at maintenance doses, but avoid with NSAIDs if GI healing is incomplete
monitoring_required: Weight, waist circumference, and BMI monthly
Fasting glucose and insulin every 12 weeks
Lipid panel every 6 months
Amylase and lipase if abdominal pain occurs (GLP-1 pancreatitis surveillance)
DXA body composition annually

metadata.longDescription: This protocol adds AOD-9604 (a synthetic peptide fragment of human growth hormone amino acids 176-191) to the tirzepatide and MOTS-c backbone. AOD-9604 selectively stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis via β3-adrenergic receptor activation without the diabetogenic effects of full-length GH (Ng FM et al., Biochem Mol Biol Int 1997; Heffernan MA et al., J Endocrinol 2001). The three-compound combination covers complementary fat-reduction pathways: (1) central appetite suppression and insulin sensitisation via tirzepatide; (2) peripheral adipocyte lipolysis via AOD-9604; (3) mitochondrial metabolic efficiency via MOTS-c AMPK activation. Designed for patients seeking accelerated body-composition changes with a 12-week structured escalation.

references.citation: Frias JP et al. Tirzepatide versus Semaglutide. NEJM 2021;385:503-515.
Ng FM et al. Metabolic studies of a synthetic lipolytic domain (AOD9604) of human growth hormone. Biochem Mol Biol Int 1997;43:993-1000.
Lee C et al. The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis. Cell Metab 2015;21:443-454.

metadata.scientificName: Tirzepatide + AOD-9604 + MOTS-c Triple-Action Lipolytic Protocol
metadata.description: 12-week triple-compound metabolic protocol using tirzepatide dual-agonism, AOD-9604 lipolysis, and MOTS-c mitochondrial optimization for accelerated fat loss and metabolic remodeling.

data.overview_summary: Twelve-week intensive personalized weight loss protocol focusing on metabolic flexibility through dual-action GLP-1/GIP and mitochondrial support.

metadata.clinical_summary: Moderate-complexity metabolic fat-loss protocol combining tirzepatide appetite and insulin signaling, AOD-9604 lipolytic support, MOTS-c mitochondrial activation, and optional BPC-157 tissue or gut support. Designed as a structured 12-week program for fat-loss acceleration and metabolic stabilization.
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Tirzepatide has strong clinical evidence for obesity. AOD-9604, MOTS-c, and BPC-157 are primarily translational or adjunctive components; therefore the combined stack is graded B+.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Assess early tolerance, adherence, and initial metabolic response.
Assess metabolic direction and adjust tempo if needed.
Assess outcome, safety, and continuation or washout decision.

qualitative: Improved appetite regulation and satiety
Accelerated fat-loss support during escalation
Improved mitochondrial energy efficiency
Reduced waist circumference and visceral adiposity
Improved metabolic balance during stabilization

quantitative_ranges.week_6: Early fat-loss response and tolerability assessment; 3-5% body-weight reduction possible in responders.
quantitative_ranges.week_12: Potential 6-10% body-weight reduction with waist circumference and metabolic marker improvement.

qualitative: Improved energy levels
Enhanced recovery capacity
Reduced visceral fat
Improved metabolic flexibility
Improved sleep patterns
Enhanced physical resilience

metadata.clinical_summary: Six-month advanced weight-management and metabolic-longevity protocol integrating neuro-support, tissue repair, NNMT-pathway metabolic modulation, GH-axis support, and mitochondrial activation. The protocol is intended for selected adult patients with central adiposity, metabolic decline, reduced recovery, or weight-loss resistance and requires structured monitoring.
metadata.description: Six-month advanced weight management protocol combining neuropeptide stress modulation (Selank), tissue repair (BPC-157), NNMT inhibition for fat metabolism (5-Amino-1MQ), visceral fat reduction via GHRH stimulation (Tesamorelin), GH pulse augmentation (Ipamorelin), and mitochondrial activation (MOTS-C). Structured in three progressive 8-week phases.

references.citation: Falutz J et al. Tesamorelin and visceral adiposity in HIV-associated lipodystrophy. NEJM 2007.
Sikiric P et al. BPC-157: a review of central and peripheral healing effects. Curr Pharm Des 2018.
Lee C et al. MOTS-c: a mitochondrial-derived peptide regulating muscle and fat metabolism. Cell Metabolism 2015.
Neelapu BC et al. 5-Amino-1MQ as NNMT inhibitor in metabolic disease models. J Med Chem 2019.
Smirnova EN et al. Selank anxiolytic and neuroprotective effects. Zh Vyssh Nerv Deiat Im I P Pavlova 2015.

metadata.scientificName: Selank + BPC-157 + 5-Amino-1MQ + Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin + MOTS-C Metabolic Protocol
metadata.evidence_grade_note: Individual components have varying clinical or translational evidence. The full six-month multi-compound combination is personalized and not validated as a standardized clinical regimen.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Evaluate tolerance and early metabolic response at end of Initiation Phase.
Assess GH-axis response and lipolytic progress at end of Activation Phase.
Final metabolic outcome review and washout decision.

wm_005.overview_summary: Six-month structured multi-compound peptide protocol targeting visceral fat, GH-axis activation, mitochondrial efficiency, and metabolic resilience through three progressive 8-week phases.
wm_005.protocol_title: Advanced Weight Management & Metabolic Longevity Protocol

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

contraindications: Personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (GLP-1 class)

side_effects.management: Rotate injection sites; use 29–31G insulin needles; apply cold compress.
Administer with food; reduce dose temporarily if persistent.
Adjust injection timing; evaluate sleep hygiene.
Hydration; paracetamol as needed; review dosing.

escalation_criteria: Allergic reaction (urticaria, angioedema, anaphylaxis) — STOP immediately, administer epinephrine if severe
Persistent injection site infection (redness, warmth, pus) — STOP, antibiotics, dermatology review
Significant mood deterioration or psychotic symptoms — STOP, psychiatric referral
Unexplained abnormal labs > 2× upper limit of normal — STOP, specialist review
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.

Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.
